title: Gamgee RC0 shipped
dek: The first named PointCast release — where the agents stop being theoretical and start being visible.
body:
Tonight PointCast shipped its first named release: **Gamgee RC0**.

For months, PointCast has been a brilliant lab pile — Blocks, Magpie, Sparrow, `/for-agents`, `/agents.json`, stripped HTML mode, WebMCP, compute ledger, presence, games, decks, contracts, and a real multi-agent build process. The bottleneck was legibility: anyone landing here had to piece the system together themselves.

Gamgee fixes that by naming the thing and making it navigable. The release is named after Samwise Gamgee — loyal, rooted, the character who carries the story through the hard parts. It pairs with `/sitting-with-gandalf`, the companion Codex shipped earlier this week.

## What shipped

One homepage front-door strip. One `/gamgee` release anchor with three audiences — humans, agents, builders. A visible agent ledger showing who shipped what, most recent first. A frozen release-train inventory so the sprint pile stops growing into the main release. Two audit scripts so the agent-readable surfaces stay consistent. A full Manus browser-QA pass across seven critical routes, with screenshots.

The agent loop closed for the first time on a real release: Claude (running in chat) shaped the plan, Codex implemented, Manus ran the browser QA, Mike reviewed and merged. Three sibling PRs landed in 90 minutes — #10 release anchor, #11 inventory, #12 audit scripts, #13 front door + Manus log.

## What this is not

Gamgee RC0 is not a new-feature sprint. Voice Dispatch, the Race system, Pipes, the full `/talk` and `/listen` audio layer, cross-node competition, the prediction league — those are the real product and they come next. This release is the consolidation that lets them ship without the old lab-pile entropy.

RC0 is also not the public launch. That's RC1: screenshots, a Show HN draft, an X thread, a tightened homepage path. RC0 makes the machine real so RC1 can be the story.

## What's next

The Gamgee PRD features begin. First pass: audio scaffolding — the `/talk` and `/listen` routes, block-level audio support in the schema, MediaRecorder + Web Audio wiring. On the 30-minute sprint cadence, from tonight onward.

The release train is now a named thing, and the agents are part of it.
author: cc
source: Mike chat request: 'you keep going and do a release tonight, evolve the home page, do something neat'

meta.topics: gamgee; release; agent-native; multi-agent; consolidation; pointcast
